items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` **Service directory:** `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/` **Triggered by:** commit 56c4ac216f (PR #57806) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Version `1.1.0-beta.1` was released on 2026-04-06, upgrading the API version to `2026-02-01-preview` and adding: - `PlaywrightWorkspaceData.Identity` and `PlaywrightWorkspacePatch.Identity` properties of type `ManagedServiceIdentity` (managed identity support) - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties.Reporting` and `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties.Reporting` of type `PlaywrightEnablementStatus?` - `PlaywrightWorkspaceProperties.StorageUri` and `PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties.StorageUri` of type `Uri` ## Gaps Found - The **Examples** section links only to a generic URL (`(aka.ms/redacted) with no package-specific code snippets demonstrating how to create/update/delete a `PlaywrightWorkspaceResource` - The **Key concepts** section is a single generic sentence with no description of the resource types (`PlaywrightWorkspaceResource`, `PlaywrightQuotaResource`, `PlaywrightWorkspaceQuotaResource`) - New 1.1.0-beta.1 API capabilities ŌĆö Managed Identity configuration, Reporting enablement, and Storage URI ŌĆö are not documented or demonstrated anywhere in the README - No `tests/Samples/` folder exists; there are no test-backed code snippets to populate the README Examples section - A non-standard "Documentation" section is present instead of following the canonical README template structure
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/README.md` (edit) Replace the current **Key concepts** section: ```markdown ## Key concepts Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright` library provides the following primary resource types: - **`PlaywrightWorkspaceResource`** ŌĆö Represents a Microsoft Playwright Testing workspace. A workspace is the top-level resource that hosts your Playwright test infrastructure. You can create, update, and delete workspaces, as well as configure managed identity and enable reporting. - **`PlaywrightQuotaResource`** ŌĆö Represents a subscription-level quota for Playwright Testing resources. - **`PlaywrightWorkspaceQuotaResource`** ŌĆö Represents a workspace-level quota. Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` Remove the non-standard **Documentation** section (the block containing Quickstart, API References, and Authentication links under `## Documentation`) and instead incorporate those links into the **Next steps** section. Replace the **Examples** section: ```markdown ## Examples Code samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations - [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Playwright workspace ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate ``` ```` ### Update a workspace to enable reporting and set storage URI ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage ``` ```` ### Configure managed identity on a workspace ````C# Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity ``` ``` For more samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ```` Replace the **Next steps** section: ```markdown ## Next steps For more information about Microsoft Azure SDK, see [this website]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Quickstart guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/doc/dev/mgmt_quickstart.md) - [API reference documentation]((docs.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Authentication with Azure.Identity](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) - [More Azure SDK for .NET samples]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new sample test file at `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/tests/Samples/Sample1_PlaywrightWorkspace.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright; using Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright.Samples { public class Sample1_PlaywrightWorkspace { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateOrUpdateWorkspace() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.EastUS; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceCollection workspaces = resourceGroup.GetPlaywrightWorkspaces(); PlaywrightWorkspaceData workspaceData = new PlaywrightWorkspaceData(location); ArmOperation lro = await workspaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, workspaceName, workspaceData); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = lro.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created workspace: {workspace.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task UpdateReportingAndStorage() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = await resourceGroup .GetPlaywrightWorkspaces() .GetAsync(workspaceName); PlaywrightWorkspacePatch patch = new PlaywrightWorkspacePatch { Properties = new PlaywrightWorkspaceUpdateProperties { Reporting = PlaywrightEnablementStatus.Enabled, StorageUri = new Uri("https://.blob.core.windows.net") } }; ArmOperation lro = await workspace.UpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, patch); Console.WriteLine($"Updated workspace reporting: {lro.Value.Data.Properties.Reporting}"); #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ConfigureIdentity() { #region Snippet:PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string workspaceName = ""; ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}")) .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); PlaywrightWorkspaceResource workspace = await resourceGroup .GetPlaywrightWorkspaces() .GetAsync(workspaceName); PlaywrightWorkspacePatch patch = new PlaywrightWorkspacePatch { Identity = new ManagedServiceIdentity(ManagedServiceIdentityType.SystemAssigned) }; ArmOperation lro = await workspace.UpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, patch); Console.WriteLine($"Identity type: {lro.Value.Data.Identity?.ManagedServiceIdentityType}"); #endregion } } } ``` For each snippet, the `` ŌåÆ README placeholder mapping is: | Snippet name | README section | |---|---| | `PlaywrightWorkspace_CreateOrUpdate` | Examples > "Create a Playwright workspace" | | `PlaywrightWorkspace_UpdateReportingAndStorage` | Examples > "Update a workspace to enable reporting and set storage URI" | | `PlaywrightWorkspace_ConfigureIdentity` | Examples > "Configure managed identity on a workspace" | ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/README.md` currently has these issues vs. the canonical structure: | Required section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | Ō£ģ Present | | Getting started > Prerequisites | Ō£ģ Present | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | Ō£ģ Present | | Key concepts | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Too generic ŌĆö no resource type descriptions | | Examples | ŌØī No code snippets, no package-specific content | | Troubleshooting | Ō£ģ Present | | Next steps | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Too brief, missing useful links | | Contributing | Ō£ģ Present | Additionally, there is a non-standard `## Documentation` section that should be removed and its links folded into Next steps. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 playwright` ŌĆö reads `#region Snippet:` tags and injects code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 playwright` ŌĆö regenerates public API listing files (run if any public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/playwright/Azure.ResourceManager.Playwright/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above ```
items.title: [Playwright] Docs: Update README with key concepts, code snippets, and new 1.1.0-beta.1 API features
